title: Newsletter actionability score consistently below 0.5 — synthesis prompt lacks actionable-insight instructions
description: The quality_history table shows actionability scores at or below 0.5 on 9 of the last 14 days (0.3 on 03/25, 0.2 on 03/15, 0.5 on most other days). The overall_score is often dragged down primarily by this dimension while coverage, dedup, and length_score are consistently 0.8-1.0. This indicates the synthesis/newsletter writer is reporting news but not adding 'so what' / 'what to do about it' framing. The research findings include summaries but the synthesis prompt does not explicitly require actionable takeaways or builder-oriented advice for each section.
requirements: Add explicit actionability instructions to the synthesis prompt (e.g. 'For each major story, include one concrete action readers can take')
Add section-level actionability check in the evaluator scoring logic
Set a quality floor: if actionability < 0.5, flag for revision before delivery
done_criteria: Actionability score averages >= 0.65 over a 7-day window
Synthesis prompt includes explicit instructions for actionable takeaways
Evaluator logs a warning when actionability drops below 0.5
expected_impact: Raises actionability score from ~0.5 average to ~0.7+ by adding explicit synthesis prompt instructions and evaluation feedback loop
context: quality_history data: actionability scores of 0.7, 0.7, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.8, 1.0, 0.3, 0.7, 0.7, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.8 over last 14 days. This is the weakest quality dimension by far. Other dimensions (coverage, dedup, length, topic_balance) are consistently 0.85-1.0.
